{"query":"xAI rebrand SpaceXAI SpaceX AI division announcement date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":814,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/spacexai-xai-rebrand-grok-what-it-means-2026","title":"SpaceXAI: Inside xAI's Rebrand and What It Signals","content":"On July 6, 2026, xAI changed its name, logo and X handle to SpaceXAI.
It reads like a rebrand, but the ownership change already happened five months earlier.
The July 6 announcement changed the name and brand — nothing about the legal structure, the ownership, or which company controls the Colossus data center moved that day.
Elon Musk had actually telegraphed the rename months earlier, posting in May 2026 that xAI would "be dissolved as a separate company." July 6 was the execution of that intent, not a new corporate event.
When SpaceX went public on the Nasdaq on June 12, 2026, it priced at $135 per share for a $1.77 trillion valuation and raised roughly $75 billion.
As of the S-1's March 31, 2026 disclosure period.
Read that through: the compute business may be worth more as neutral ground than the model shop is as a competitor.
07 — The ThesisThe strategic rationale is orbital AI compute.
SpaceX's acquisition of xAI closed on February 2, 2026, more than five months before the rebrand."},{"url":"https://www.businessinsider.com/xai-rebrand-spacexai-new-logo-x-handle-spacex-2026-7","title":"XAI Rebrands to SpaceXAI With New Logo, X Handle, Under SpaceX - Business Insider","content":"The account also shared a video of the xAI logo getting folded into a new SpaceXAI logo.
Musk said in May that xAI would be dissolved as a separate company and folded into SpaceX, with the company's AI products branded as SpaceXAI.
The rebrand comes after SpaceX's blockbuster IPO in June.
The company has also landed some big AI infrastructure deals, with Anthropic agreeing to pay SpaceX $1.25 billion a month for access to compute power at its Colossus data centers and Google agreeing to pay $920 million a month.
In addition, you accept Insider’s Terms of Service and Privacy Policy."},{"url":"https://techjacksolutions.com/ai-brief/xai-rebrands-spacexai-developer-enterprise-compute/","title":"xAI Rebrands SpaceXAI: AI Data Center News","content":"July 7, 2026 3 min read Reuters Partial Strong X
xAI has reportedly rebranded to SpaceXAI, following SpaceX's all-stock acquisition of xAI in early 2026.
The consolidation places Grok-family models, the Colossus GPU infrastructure, and SpaceX's satellite network under a single entity, with significant implications for developers and enterprises that depend on that stack.
SpaceX reportedly directed ~$12.7B toward AI infrastructure in 2025; Google reportedly pays ~$920M/month for Colossus GPU access, both figures are T3-sourced and require independent confirmation
Even directionally, the structure describes a compute supplier with pricing power that rivals hyperscaler cloud divisions.
Together, the pattern suggests SpaceX is assembling an integrated AI stack: model development (xAI/Grok), coding tooling (Cursor), and raw compute infrastructure (Colossus)."},{"url":"https://coinpaper.com/16930/elon-musk-s-space-x-rebrands-x-ai-to-space-xai-for-orbital-computing-ahead-of-1-75-t-ipo","title":"SpaceX Rebrands xAI to SpaceXAI for Orbital Computing Amid $1.75T IPO","content":"Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI is being folded into SpaceX under a new brand, SpaceXAI, as the aerospace company expands its ambitions in artificial intelligence, satellite-based data centres and orbital computing infrastructure.
He also said the new AI unit would have its own logo.
SpaceX has already discussed plans for space-based computing systems.
Musk had previously criticized Anthropic in public posts, but later said he had met senior members of the team and was impressed by their competence and stated concern for safety."}]}

SpaceXAI is the new public branding around xAI after SpaceX acquired the AI company on February 2, 2026. The July 6, 2026 @SpaceXAI post is a rebrand signal, not proof by itself of a new model, new API endpoint, or immediate product migration. **SpaceXAI** is the name now showing up around xAI’s public brand after SpaceX acquired xAI earlier in 2026. The new signal is simple but important: on **July 6, 2026**, the linked X account posted, “We are now @SpaceXAI.” That does not mean every xAI product, API endpoint, invoice, legal entity, or developer integration changed overnight. The official acquisition announcement came on **February 2, 2026**, when xAI said SpaceX had acquired xAI. For users, the visible products are still Grok, Grok Build, Imagine, Voice, Grokipedia, and the xAI API. | Is SpaceXAI a new AI model? | No. Grok is the AI assistant/model product; SpaceXAI is the broader company/brand.","score":0.89302075,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SpaceXAI","title":"SpaceXAI","content":"**SpaceXAI** (formerly **xAI**), is a subsidiary of the American spaceflight company SpaceX working in the areas of artificial intelligence (AI) and social media. SpaceXAI's flagship products are the generative AI chatbot Grok \"Grok (chatbot)\") and the social network X \"X (social network)\"), which was acquired in March 2025. Prior to being acquired by SpaceX in February 2026, SpaceXAI's assets were part of a standalone corporation named X.AI Corp., founded by Elon Musk and 11 researchers in 2023. Elon Musk founded xAI on March 9, 2023. Musk announced xAI on July 12, 2023. On March 28, 2025, Musk announced that xAI acquired sister company X Corp., the developer of social media platform X (social network) \"X (social network)\"), which was previously acquired by Musk in October 2022. In May 2026, Musk announced that xAI would cease to exist as a separate company, with Grok and X now being the AI division of SpaceX.","score":0.8742601,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/spacexai-xai-rebrand-grok-what-it-means-2026","title":"SpaceXAI: Inside xAI's Rebrand and What It Signals","content":"# xAI Is Now*SpaceXAI*: What the Rebrand Actually Signals. On July 6, 2026, xAI changed its name, logo and X handle to SpaceXAI. What actually shifted on July 6 is the signal it sends to anyone building on Grok: you are no longer betting on an independent AI lab. On July 6, 2026, xAI became SpaceXAI. The company changed its name, unveiled a new logo that nests the \"xAI\" letters inside the SpaceX identity, and switched its X account to @SpaceXAI. The acquisition that put Grok, X and the Colossus data center under SpaceX had already closed on February 2, 2026. Dropping \"xAI\" as a standalone brand in favor of \"SpaceXAI\" positions Grok the way Starlink is positioned: as a product line of a much larger company, not an independent AI lab with its own roadmap, its own capital allocation, and its own uptime priorities.","score":0.8706085,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/Dai0Jrfnb-_","title":"Instagram","content":"Sign up for Instagram to stay in the loop.
